name: Big White Soft Sugar Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. In a measuring cup, mix the buttermilk and baking soda; set aside.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine the oil, sugar, and eggs; mix well.
3. Add the buttermilk-baking soda mixture to the oil-sugar-egg mixture and blend.
4. Gradually add the salt, nutmeg, vanilla, and baking powder, then mix again.
5. Gently fold in the flour (the batter will be very runny). Cover and refrigerate overnight.
6. Preheat oven to 400°F. For best results, use ungreased non-stick baking sheets.
7. Lightly grease a baking sheet with oil if needed.
8. Use about 1 heaping tablespoon of batter per cookie, placing them on the prepared sheet.
9. Sprinkle each cookie generously with sugar and dot with 3 raisins.
10. Bake for 5 minutes. The cookies should be just barely done—still almost white. If they are golden, remove from oven.
11. Allow cookies to remain on the baking sheet for an additional 3 minutes to finish baking.
12. Carefully transfer cookies using a metal spatula to a wax paper-covered rack to cool.
13. Store in tightly covered containers or freeze. Wrap each cookie individually or between layers of wax paper before storing.
